title: Mapping Context to Output
concept: Adding a linear dense layer and sigmoid activation to map the final LSTM state to a binary probability.

exercises.prompt: What is an LSTM used for in sentiment analysis?

options.text: To learn from sequences of text

exercises.explain: An LSTM learns patterns in sequences, which is perfect for analyzing the meaning in text.

cards.title: Mapping Outputs
cards.body: To turn information from an LSTM into a recommendation or a decision, we use an output layer.

cards.title: Linear Dense Layer
cards.body: A linear dense layer takes input data and transforms it through weighted sums, like a formula that combines the inputs in a specific way.

cards.title: Activation Function
cards.body: Think of the sigmoid activation as a light switch; it helps turn on or off whether a piece of information should be used to decide.

cards.title: LSTM to Sigmoid Flow
cards.body: After processing words, the LSTM gives a final output that flows into the dense layer, where it is transformed into a probability.

cards.title: Myth: More Layers are Always Better
cards.body: More layers are not always better; having the right layers and connections is what matters for mapping outputs correctly.

cards.title: Model Structure
cards.body: The LSTM outputs a final state that then feeds into a dense layer and sigmoid to produce a binary output.

exercises.prompt: What does the final LSTM state connect to in our model?
exercises.explain: The final LSTM state provides information to the sigmoid function to determine the output probability.

exercises.prompt: The sigmoid function converts linear output into a probability score.
exercises.explain: True! The sigmoid function maps the output to a value between 0 and 1, representing a probability.

exercises.prompt: A linear dense layer uses weighted sums to transform the _____ from the LSTM into a probability.
exercises.sentence: A linear dense layer uses weighted sums to transform the final output from the LSTM into a probability.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'final output', which is derived from the LSTM before being processed.

exercises.prompt: Which step directly follows the LSTM in creating output probabilities?

options.text: It sends the data to a print function.
It connects to a linear dense layer and sigmoid activation.
It gets stored in a database.

exercises.explain: The data flows to a linear dense layer and sigmoid activation next, which helps in generating the output probabilities.

exercises.prompt: What role does a linear dense layer and sigmoid play in mapping context to output?

options.text: It stores previous data points for backup.
It transforms LSTM output into a binary probability.
It visualizes data trends across multiple graphs.
It does nothing and lets the model run on its own.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that it transforms the LSTM's output so that the model can make a clear decision as a probability between two choices.
